rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Intervenors on behalf of their suspended insured defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Ms. Morris was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Scott Co. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages and causes of action for false representation and intentional tort is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable for punitive damages, false representation or intentional tort. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
